item.title: Eno — A Year with Swollen Appendices
item.description: Author: Brian Eno Title: A Year with Swollen Appendices: Brian Eno’s Diary Year: 1996 Type: Diary and essays (Faber and Faber; 25th-anniversary edition 2021) The shelf’s primary source is a pocket diary that outgrew itself.

item.title: Szabo — Turn On, Tune In, Drift Off
item.description: Author: Victor Szabo Title: Turn On, Tune In, Drift Off: Ambient Music’s Psychedelic Past Year: 2022 Type: Academic book (Oxford University Press; paperback 2023) The genre’s academic history arrived arguing with the founding myth.

item.title: Sheppard — On Some Faraway Beach
item.description: Author: David Sheppard Title: On Some Faraway Beach: The Life and Times of Brian Eno Year: 2008 Type: Biography (Orion; revised White Rabbit edition 2024) The founding figure got his biography from a fellow musician.

item.title: Paul McCartney
item.description: EraBritish Invasion (with the Beatles, 1963–1970); solo, 1970–presentInstrumentBass, vocals, piano, guitar, drumsGenresPop rockSinger-songwriterArt popSoft rockFolk popBandsThe BeatlesWingsThe FiremanMovementBritish beat boomListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify Paul McCartney plays the bass like a second s...

item.title: Dance
item.description: RYM On Valentine’s Day 1970, David Mancuso needed rent money and threw a party at his loft on 647 Broadway: about a hundred guests, two dollars and fifty cents at the door with food and punch included, a sound system assembled like a hi-fi obsessive’s living room (because it was one), no liquor lic...
